questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer Mr. Ramesh Gupta has complained that his net banking access has been blocked for 3 days. Write a formal email explaining the reason (scheduled maintenance) and the resolution timeline.
questions.modelAnswer:
To:rameshgupta@email.com
Subject: Temporary Unavailability of Net Banking Services

Dear Mr. Ramesh Gupta
Thank you for contacting us regarding your net banking issue. We regret the inconvenience caused due to the temporary blockage of your net banking access.
Your account has been affected because of scheduled maintenance of our online banking system. This maintenance is being carried out to improve the security and performance of our services. As a result, some customers have been unable to access net banking during this period.
Our technical team is working to complete the maintenance as quickly as possible. We expect your net banking access to be restored within the next 24 hours. You can then log in using your existing User ID and password.
We appreciate your patience and understanding. If the issue continues after the mentioned time, please reply to this email or contact our customer care for further assistance.
Regards,

Pawan Sharma
Probationary Officer (PO)

questions.text: You are a PO at a semi-urban SBI branch. An elderly woman, Mrs. Kamla Devi (72), reports that she shared her OTP with someone posing as SBI customer care and ₹45,000 was debited. She is in tears. Describe the steps you will take immediately.
questions.modelAnswer:
As a Probationary Officer, I would first remain calm and listen to Mrs. Kamla Devi's concern regarding the fraudulent transaction patiently. I would comfort her, express empathy, and assure her that I would take immediate steps to help. 

First, I would block the affected account or debit card, if required, to prevent any further unauthorized transactions. Next, I would verify the transaction details and register a fraud complaint as per the bank's guidelines. 

I would immediately inform the concerned department and guide her in reporting the incident through the appropriate fraud reporting channels. I would also advise her to file a police complaint, if necessary. 

Throughout the process, I would keep her informed about the progress and explain every step in simple language. 

Finally, I would educate her about OTP safety and other cyber fraud precautions. This approach would ensure prompt action, customer satisfaction, and protect the bank's interests.

questions.text: You are Manager of a Bank, and you conducted a customer connect camp for account opening of economically weaker sections of public. Write a Report to your Head Office on the camp.
questions.modelAnswer:
Date: February 7, 2025
To: Head Office
From: Amit Sharma, Branch Manager

Subject: Report on Customer Connect Camp for Account Opening

Dear Sir/Madam,
I am pleased to submit a report on the successful Customer Connect Camp conducted at our branch aimed at facilitating account openings for economically weaker sections of the community. The camp was held on February 5, 2025, and focused on offering easy access to banking services to individuals from underserved backgrounds.
The event was strategically organized to cater to people who may not have access to traditional banking facilities or find the account opening process cumbersome. The camp was held at a local community hall and was attended by approximately 150 individuals. We provided on-the-spot assistance for account openings, explaining the benefits of savings accounts, financial inclusion, and digital banking.
Our team was on hand to guide attendees through the process, ensuring that all documentation requirements were clear and easily understandable. Special attention was given to the elderly, women, and first-time account holders. In addition, our staff also shared information on government schemes such as Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana and various subsidy programs.
By the end of the camp, we successfully opened 120 new accounts and raised awareness about the importance of financial inclusion. Feedback from participants has been overwhelmingly positive, with many appreciating the ease and convenience provided during the event.
We look forward to continuing such initiatives to further promote financial inclusion.

Best regards,
Amit Sharma
Branch Manager
Pre-Recruitment Branch

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in 80-100 words.

"In recent years, technology has transformed the way people work, learn, and communicate. Smartphones, high-speed internet, and digital platforms have made information available within seconds. Students can attend online classes, professionals can work remotely, and businesses can reach customers across the globe. These developments have improved convenience and increased productivity in many sectors. However, excessive dependence on technology has also created several challenges that cannot be ignored.

One major concern is the decline in face-to-face interaction. People often spend more time on social media than engaging with family and friends, leading to weaker personal relationships. Another issue is the spread of misinformation. False news can circulate rapidly on digital platforms, influencing public opinion before facts are verified. Cybersecurity has also become a growing concern as individuals and organizations face threats such as data theft, identity fraud, and online scams. Furthermore, prolonged screen time has been linked to health problems, including eye strain, poor posture, and reduced physical activity.

Despite these drawbacks, technology itself is not the problem. The real challenge lies in how it is used. Responsible digital behaviour, critical thinking, and awareness of online risks can help individuals enjoy the benefits of technology while minimizing its negative effects. Governments, educational institutions, and technology companies must work together to promote digital literacy and strengthen cybersecurity measures. By using technology wisely and maintaining a healthy balance between online and offline life, society can continue to benefit from innovation without compromising personal well-being or social values."
questions.modelAnswer: Technology has made life easier by improving communication, education, and work. It helps people save time and increases productivity. However, overuse of technology can reduce personal interaction, spread false information, increase cybercrime, and cause health problems. These issues can be controlled through responsible use, digital awareness, and better online safety. Governments, schools, and technology companies should promote digital literacy. A balanced use of technology can help people enjoy its benefits while protecting their health and social values.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Digital Banking and Financial Inclusion: Opportunities and Challenges in India."
questions.modelAnswer:
India has witnessed a remarkable shift towards digital banking over the past decade, driven by initiatives such as the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), and the spread of affordable smartphones and internet connectivity. This transformation has opened up unprecedented opportunities to extend formal financial services to previously unbanked populations, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas.

UPI alone processes billions of transactions every month, allowing even small vendors and farmers to receive payments instantly without visiting a branch. Digital lending platforms have made credit accessible to small businesses lacking traditional collateral, while mobile-based insurance and investment products have widened the reach of financial planning to first-time users.

However, several challenges limit the full potential of this shift. Digital literacy remains uneven, with many elderly and rural users struggling to navigate banking apps confidently. Cybersecurity threats, including phishing and OTP fraud, disproportionately affect first-time digital users unfamiliar with safe practices. Connectivity gaps in remote regions further continue to exclude a significant section of the population.

To address this, banks must invest in vernacular-language interfaces, simplified user experiences, and continuous financial literacy campaigns run with local self-help groups and Business Correspondents. Strengthening cybersecurity awareness and grievance redressal is equally important to build trust.

In conclusion, while digital banking has significantly advanced financial inclusion in India, true inclusion will only be achieved when access is matched by usage, trust and safety — a balance of technology, education and protection.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has emerged as one of the most successful digital payment innovations in the world, processing billions of transactions every month across India. Launched in 2016 by the National Payments Corporation of India, UPI allows users to transfer money instantly between bank accounts using just a mobile number or a virtual payment address, without sharing sensitive banking details. Its real-time nature, zero transaction cost for small payments, and interoperability across banks and apps have made it the preferred mode of payment for millions of Indians, from large retailers to roadside vendors. The success of UPI has drawn international attention, with several countries exploring similar real-time payment systems or partnering with India to enable UPI-based transactions for tourists and businesses abroad. However, experts caution that the rapid growth of digital payments must be matched by stronger fraud prevention measures, as cybercriminals increasingly target inexperienced users through fake payment requests and phishing links."

(a) What does UPI allow users to do, and what information is required to make a transfer? (2 marks)
(b) Name two factors that have made UPI the preferred mode of payment in India. (2 marks)
(c) How has UPI's success been recognised beyond India's borders? (2 marks)
(d) What concern do experts raise regarding the rapid growth of digital payments? (2 marks)
(e) Find one word from the passage that means "the quality of being able to work together across different systems." (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) UPI allows users to transfer money instantly between bank accounts using only a mobile number or virtual payment address, without sharing sensitive banking details.
(b) Its real-time nature and zero transaction cost for small payments, along with interoperability across banks and apps, have made it the preferred payment mode.
(c) Several countries are exploring similar real-time payment systems or partnering with India to enable UPI-based transactions for tourists and businesses abroad.
(d) Experts caution that growth must be matched by stronger fraud prevention measures, as cybercriminals target inexperienced users through fake payment requests and phishing links.
(e) Interoperability.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Artificial Intelligence in Banking: Transforming Customer Service and Risk Management."
questions.modelAnswer:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly reshaping the banking sector, fundamentally altering how financial institutions interact with customers and manage risk. From chatbots handling routine queries around the clock to algorithms detecting fraudulent transactions in real time, AI has become central to modern banking operations.

In customer service, AI-powered virtual assistants now resolve a large share of routine queries instantly, freeing human staff to focus on complex grievances and relationship management. Personalised product recommendations based on a customer's transaction history have improved cross-selling while enhancing satisfaction. Voice and chat interfaces in regional languages are also making banking more accessible to non-English speaking customers.

On the risk management front, AI models analyse vast volumes of transaction data to identify unusual patterns indicative of fraud, often flagging suspicious activity within seconds — far faster than traditional rule-based systems. Credit scoring has similarly benefited, with machine learning models incorporating alternative data such as utility payments to assess creditworthiness for customers without a formal credit history.

Despite these benefits, AI adoption raises concerns. Algorithmic bias can disadvantage certain customer segments if training data is not carefully curated. Over-reliance on automated decisions may reduce human oversight in sensitive cases, while data privacy remains a persistent challenge as more customer information is processed.

Going forward, banks must adopt AI responsibly — ensuring transparency, regular algorithm audits, and robust data protection. Deployed thoughtfully, AI can make banking more efficient, inclusive and secure for millions of customers across India.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Climate finance has become a critical pillar of India's strategy to meet its net-zero emissions target by 2070. Banks and financial institutions are increasingly directing credit towards renewable energy projects, electric vehicle infrastructure, and sustainable agriculture, often supported by concessional lending rates and government-backed guarantee schemes. The Reserve Bank of India has encouraged banks to factor climate-related financial risks into their lending decisions, recognising that extreme weather events can affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in agriculture-dependent regions. Green bonds, which raise funds specifically for environmentally beneficial projects, have gained popularity among public sector banks and private corporations seeking to diversify funding sources while demonstrating environmental commitment. Despite this progress, experts note that climate finance in India remains a fraction of what is required to meet stated targets, and call for clearer regulatory frameworks, standardised green taxonomies, and greater private sector participation to bridge the funding gap."

(a) What is India's stated net-zero emissions target year? (2 marks)
(b) Name two sectors towards which banks are increasingly directing credit. (2 marks)
(c) Why has the RBI encouraged banks to factor climate-related risks into lending decisions? (2 marks)
(d) What is the purpose of green bonds, as described in the passage? (2 marks)
(e) According to experts, what is needed to bridge India's climate finance gap? (2 marks)
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) 2070.
(b) Renewable energy projects and electric vehicle infrastructure (or sustainable agriculture).
(c) Because extreme weather events can affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in agriculture-dependent regions.
(d) Green bonds raise funds specifically for environmentally beneficial projects, helping issuers diversify funding sources while showing environmental commitment.
(e) Clearer regulatory frameworks, standardised green taxonomies, and greater private sector participation.

questions.text: A customer's credit card application has been rejected due to a low CIBIL score. Write an email to the applicant explaining the reason for rejection and the steps they can take to improve eligibility for future applications.

A low credit score is often the result of high existing credit utilisation, delayed EMI payments, or limited credit history. We encourage you to review your credit report, clear any outstanding dues, and maintain timely repayments on existing loans.

[Your Name]

questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer disputes an international transaction of $450 on his debit card that he claims he never made, while travelling abroad his card details may have been compromised. Describe the steps you will take immediately.
questions.modelAnswer:
As a Probationary Officer, I would first remain calm and listen to the customer's concern regarding the disputed international transaction carefully. I would understand the issue, express empathy, and assure the customer that I would provide all possible assistance. 

First, I would block the debit card immediately to prevent any further unauthorized transactions. Next, I would verify the transaction details and register the customer's complaint as per the bank's guidelines. 

I would guide the customer through the card dispute process and coordinate with the concerned department for further investigation. If required, I would advise the customer to apply for a replacement debit card. 

Throughout the process, I would keep the customer informed about the complaint status and the expected timeline for resolution. 

Finally, I would advise the customer to monitor account activity regularly and follow safe banking practices while travelling abroad. This approach would ensure timely resolution, maintain customer confidence, and protect the bank's interests.

questions.text: Two customers get into a heated argument in the branch lobby over queue jumping, disturbing other customers waiting for service. As the PO on duty, describe how you would handle the situation.
questions.modelAnswer: As a Probationary Officer, I would first remain calm and immediately intervene to handle the argument between the two customers. I would politely request both customers to remain calm and listen to their concerns separately to understand the reason for the dispute. After verifying the facts, I would explain the bank's queue management policy and ensure that service is provided fairly without showing any bias. If required, I would request the branch staff or security personnel to assist in maintaining order. Throughout the process, I would communicate politely and ensure that other customers face minimum inconvenience. Finally, I would thank both customers for their cooperation and encourage them to follow the queue system in the future. This approach would help restore a peaceful environment, ensure fair service, maintain customer satisfaction, and uphold the bank's professional image.

questions.text: A long-standing customer visits your branch upset about poor service and says she wants to close her account and move to another bank. As the PO, describe how you would handle this situation.
questions.modelAnswer: As a Probationary Officer, I would first remain calm and listen carefully to the customer's concern regarding the poor service provided by the branch. I would request her to sit in a quiet and comfortable area, allowing her to explain her concerns without interruption. I would express sincere regret for the inconvenience caused and assure her that I would do my best to resolve the issue. Next, I would verify the facts by understanding the reason for her dissatisfaction and, if required, discussing the matter with the concerned staff. After identifying the issue, I would take appropriate corrective action as per the bank's policies and explain the available solution clearly. I would politely request her to reconsider closing her account and assure her of better service in the future. If she still decides to close the account, I would complete the process professionally and courteously. This approach would help maintain customer satisfaction, preserve the bank's reputation, and strengthen customer trust.

questions.text: An elderly, illiterate customer visits your branch to open a savings account but is unable to read or fill the application form. As the PO, describe how you would assist her.
questions.modelAnswer: As a Probationary Officer, I would first remain calm and welcome the elderly customer warmly. I would understand her difficulty in reading and filling out the savings account application form and assure her that I would provide all the necessary assistance. Next, I would verify her identity and the required documents as per the bank's guidelines. I would patiently explain the account opening process in simple language and help her fill out the application form based on the information provided by her. If required, I would arrange for her thumb impression or signature as per the prescribed procedure. I would also explain the basic features of the savings account, including passbook, ATM card, and safe banking practices. Finally, I would answer her questions and ensure that she understands the process before leaving the branch. This approach would ensure a smooth account opening process, maintain customer satisfaction, and promote inclusive banking services.

questions.text: The core banking system goes down during peak banking hours, and a long queue of frustrated customers is waiting at your branch. As the PO on duty, describe how you would manage the situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would remain calm and immediately inform customers about the technical issue and the expected resolution time, to reduce uncertainty and frustration. I would request the IT helpdesk to escalate the issue urgently.

While the system is down, I would offer to process simple cash transactions manually where possible and provide acknowledgment slips. I would arrange seating and drinking water for waiting customers and keep them updated periodically. Once the system is restored, I would prioritise customers who have been waiting longest and apologise for the inconvenience caused.

questions.text: A customer approaches you for a personal loan but does not meet the bank's minimum eligibility criteria due to a low and irregular income. As the PO, describe how you would handle this situation.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would politely explain to the customer the specific eligibility criteria he does not currently meet, such as minimum stable income requirements, in a respectful and non-judgmental manner. I would avoid raising false hopes about loan approval.

I would explore alternative options with him, such as a smaller loan amount, a co-applicant with stable income, or a gold loan if he has suitable collateral. I would also suggest he build a stronger banking relationship through regular savings, which could improve his eligibility in future. I would thank him for his interest and encourage him to stay engaged with the bank.

questions.text: A senior citizen customer is unable to use the UPI app on her phone and is anxious about how she will make digital payments at her grandson's wedding shopping. As the PO, describe how you would assist her.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would patiently explain that she can continue using her debit card or cash for purchases if UPI feels unfamiliar, so she does not feel pressured. I would then offer to demonstrate the UPI app step by step, using a sample transaction, explaining concepts like UPI PIN and QR codes in simple terms.

I would write down the steps for her in simple language to take home for reference and caution her to never share her UPI PIN or OTP with anyone, including people claiming to be bank staff. I would also share our toll-free helpline number for any future difficulty.

questions.text: A customer reports that she withdrew Rs 2,000 from an ATM but received two fake/counterfeit notes among the cash dispensed. As the PO, describe the steps you will take.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would ask the customer to provide her ATM transaction slip, the approximate time of withdrawal, and the suspected fake notes for verification. I would check the ATM's CCTV footage and cash replenishment records for that specific cassette to verify her claim.

If the claim is substantiated, I would arrange for the fake notes to be impounded as per RBI guidelines and issue her a receipt, while crediting her account with the equivalent genuine amount after due verification. I would also flag the ATM for immediate cash audit to prevent further incidents and report the matter to the currency chest.

questions.text: The branch security guard reports that he stopped a suspicious individual attempting to access the strong room area without authorisation. As the PO on duty, describe the immediate steps you would take.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would first ensure the safety of staff and customers by calmly directing the security guard to detain the individual at the entrance without use of force, while I verify the situation. I would check CCTV footage to understand how the individual gained access to that area.

I would inform the Branch Manager immediately and, depending on the severity, contact the local police to hand over the individual for further investigation. I would also file an internal incident report and recommend a review of strong room access protocols to prevent recurrence.

questions.text: You organised a Digital Banking Literacy Camp at a government degree college on 8th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager covering objectives, activities, attendance and outcomes.

The primary objective of this initiative was to educate young college students about secure digital banking channels, promote financial literacy, and raise awareness against rampant online financial frauds.

Key Activities:

The event witnessed enthusiastic participation, with approximately 210 students and 15 faculty members in attendance.

The tangible outcomes of the camp include:

This initiative has successfully strengthened our bank's footprint among the youth demographic.

Report Prepared By:
Amit Sharma
Probationary Officer
College Road Branch

questions.text: Your branch conducted an annual customer satisfaction survey covering 200 customers in May 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager summarising the methodology, key findings and recommendations.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON ANNUAL CUSTOMER SATISFACTION SURVEY

Date: 1st June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Customer Satisfaction Survey — May 2026

A structured survey covering 200 customers was conducted through in-branch questionnaires and phone calls during May 2026, assessing service quality, wait times and digital banking experience.

78% of customers rated overall service as good or excellent. However, 35% reported dissatisfaction with average waiting time during peak hours, and 20% were unaware of mobile banking features.

Recommendation: Deploy an additional counter during peak hours (11 AM-1 PM) and conduct monthly digital banking awareness sessions for walk-in customers.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in 80-100 words.

"Financial literacy is becoming an essential life skill in today's fast-changing world. Many people earn a regular income but still struggle to manage their money because they lack basic financial knowledge. They often spend without planning, borrow more than they can repay, and fail to save for future needs. As a result, they face financial stress during emergencies such as medical expenses, job loss, or unexpected family responsibilities.

Developing good financial habits from an early age can help people build a secure future. Preparing a monthly budget allows individuals to track their income and expenses. Regular saving, even in small amounts, creates a financial cushion for difficult times. Investing wisely can help money grow over the long term, while avoiding unnecessary debt reduces financial pressure. Understanding basic concepts such as interest rates, insurance, taxes, and inflation also enables people to make better financial decisions.

Technology has made financial services more accessible through digital banking and online payment platforms. However, it has also increased the risk of online fraud and financial scams. Therefore, people should verify transactions carefully, use secure passwords, and avoid sharing personal banking information with strangers.

Financial literacy is not only important for individuals but also for the overall economy. People who manage their finances responsibly are more likely to save, invest, and contribute to economic growth. Schools, families, and financial institutions should work together to spread financial awareness. Better financial knowledge helps people make informed decisions, achieve long-term goals, and lead a more stable and confident life."
questions.modelAnswer: Financial literacy helps people manage money wisely and avoid financial problems. Good habits like budgeting, saving, and careful investing help people prepare for emergencies and achieve future goals. Understanding interest rates, insurance, taxes, and inflation also supports better financial decisions. Although digital banking offers convenience, people must stay alert to online fraud. Schools, families, and financial institutions should spread financial awareness. Better financial knowledge helps individuals build a secure future and supports the country's economic growth.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in 80-100 words.

"Time is one of the most valuable resources available to every individual. Unlike money or material possessions, it cannot be earned back once it is lost. However, many people fail to use their time wisely because they postpone important tasks or become distracted by social media, entertainment, and other unnecessary activities. As deadlines approach, they experience stress and often produce work of lower quality. Poor time management also affects physical and mental well-being by reducing time for exercise, rest, and family.

Good time management begins with setting clear goals and deciding which tasks are most important. Preparing a daily schedule helps people organize their work and avoid confusion. Breaking large tasks into smaller steps makes them easier to complete and reduces the tendency to procrastinate. Taking short breaks during work can improve concentration, while avoiding unnecessary distractions increases productivity. Learning to say no to activities that do not support one's goals is another important habit.

Effective time management benefits both personal and professional life. Students who manage their time well perform better in examinations because they study regularly instead of relying on last-minute preparation. Employees who plan their work carefully are more likely to meet deadlines and earn the trust of their employers. In addition, good time management reduces stress and creates more opportunities for hobbies, relaxation, and spending time with loved ones.

Success does not depend only on talent or intelligence. It also depends on how wisely people use the limited time available to them. Those who value time are more likely to achieve their goals and enjoy a balanced and fulfilling life."
questions.modelAnswer: Time is a valuable resource that should be used wisely. Poor time management leads to stress, low-quality work, and less time for health and family. Setting clear goals, making a daily schedule, avoiding distractions, and completing tasks step by step improve productivity. Students and employees who manage their time well perform better and meet their responsibilities on time. Wise use of time helps people achieve success, reduce stress, and maintain a balanced and happy life.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in 80-100 words.

"Reading books has always been an important way of gaining knowledge and developing personality. Although digital media has become popular, books continue to play a valuable role in education and personal growth. They help readers improve their language skills, expand their vocabulary, and develop the ability to think clearly. Reading also increases concentration because it requires sustained attention, unlike short videos or social media posts that encourage constant switching between different topics.

Books expose readers to new ideas, cultures, and experiences. Biographies inspire people by sharing the struggles and achievements of successful individuals. Fiction develops creativity and imagination, while non-fiction provides practical knowledge about science, history, business, and other subjects. Regular reading also improves writing skills because readers naturally learn sentence structure, grammar, and effective ways of expressing ideas.

Despite these benefits, many people read less than before due to the growing use of smartphones and entertainment platforms. They often believe they do not have enough time to read, even though they spend several hours each day scrolling through social media. This habit reduces attention span and limits opportunities for meaningful learning.

Developing a reading habit does not require spending long hours with a book. Reading for even twenty to thirty minutes every day can make a significant difference over time. Schools, parents, and libraries should encourage children to read from an early age by providing access to interesting books and creating a positive reading environment. A strong reading habit not only improves knowledge and communication skills but also helps people become more thoughtful, confident, and lifelong learners."
questions.modelAnswer: Reading books helps people gain knowledge, improve language skills, and develop clear thinking. It also increases creativity, concentration, and writing ability. Books introduce readers to new ideas and valuable life experiences. However, many people spend more time on smartphones than reading, which affects learning and attention. Reading for a short time every day can build a strong habit. Schools and parents should encourage reading because it helps people become knowledgeable, confident, and lifelong learners.

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in 80-100 words.

"Environmental conservation has become one of the greatest challenges of the modern world. Rapid industrial growth, urbanization, and increasing consumption of natural resources have improved living standards but have also caused serious damage to the environment. Air and water pollution, deforestation, loss of biodiversity, and climate change are affecting the health and well-being of people across the world. If these problems continue unchecked, future generations may face shortages of clean water, fertile land, and other essential resources.

Protecting the environment requires the combined efforts of governments, businesses, and ordinary citizens. Governments should enforce strict environmental laws, encourage the use of renewable energy, and promote sustainable development. Industries must adopt cleaner technologies, reduce waste, and use resources responsibly. At the same time, individuals can make a significant difference through small daily actions such as saving electricity, reducing plastic use, recycling waste, planting trees, and using public transport whenever possible.

Education also plays a vital role in environmental conservation. Schools and colleges should teach students about the importance of protecting nature and encourage them to adopt eco-friendly habits from an early age. Public awareness campaigns can further inspire people to take responsibility for their surroundings.

Economic development and environmental protection should not be viewed as opposing goals. With proper planning and responsible policies, both can progress together. A healthy environment supports agriculture, public health, tourism, and overall economic growth. By working together and making sustainable choices, society can protect natural resources and ensure a better quality of life for present and future generations."
questions.modelAnswer: Environmental conservation is essential for a healthy and sustainable future. Pollution, deforestation, and overuse of natural resources are harming the environment and creating problems for future generations. Governments, industries, and individuals must work together to protect nature by reducing pollution, saving resources, and using eco-friendly practices. Education and public awareness can encourage responsible behaviour. Balancing economic development with environmental protection is possible and will help improve public health, support growth, and ensure a better future for everyone.

questions.text:

"The habit of continuous learning has become increasingly important in today's rapidly changing world. New technologies, changing industries, and evolving job requirements mean that the knowledge people gain during their formal education is often not enough for an entire career. Those who stop learning may find it difficult to adapt to new situations, while those who regularly update their skills are more likely to succeed both professionally and personally.

Continuous learning does not always require enrolling in expensive courses or earning additional degrees. People can improve their knowledge by reading books, attending workshops, watching educational videos, listening to podcasts, or learning from experienced colleagues. Even daily experiences and mistakes can become valuable lessons if individuals are willing to reflect and improve. Curiosity, discipline, and an open mind are more important than age or educational background.

Employers also value workers who are eager to learn because they can quickly adapt to new responsibilities and changing technologies. Such employees often perform better, solve problems more effectively, and contribute innovative ideas to their organizations. At the same time, continuous learning increases self-confidence by helping people develop new abilities and overcome challenges.

Educational institutions and workplaces should create an environment that encourages lifelong learning by providing training opportunities and access to learning resources. However, the responsibility for personal growth ultimately lies with each individual. Learning should not end after completing school or college but should continue throughout life. People who make learning a lifelong habit are better prepared to face change, achieve their goals, and contribute positively to society."
questions.modelAnswer: Continuous learning helps people keep up with changes in technology and work. It does not always require expensive courses, as people can learn through books, workshops, videos, and daily experiences. Employers value employees who are willing to learn because they adapt quickly and perform better. Schools and workplaces should support lifelong learning, but individuals must take responsibility for their own growth. Regular learning builds confidence, improves skills, and helps people succeed in life.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Cashless Economy: Opportunities and Challenges for India."
questions.modelAnswer:
India has made significant strides towards becoming a cashless economy, driven by the rapid adoption of UPI, mobile wallets, and government initiatives promoting digital payments. This shift offers substantial benefits, including reduced costs of printing and handling currency, improved transparency that curbs black money and tax evasion, and greater convenience for consumers and businesses alike.

For small vendors and informal businesses, digital payments have opened access to formal credit by creating a verifiable transaction history, something previously unavailable to cash-only enterprises. Government welfare transfers have also become faster and more transparent through direct digital disbursement.

However, the transition is not without challenges. A significant section of the population, particularly in rural and remote areas, still lacks reliable internet connectivity and digital literacy. Elderly and less tech-savvy citizens often struggle to adapt, risking financial exclusion if cash options are withdrawn too quickly. Cybersecurity threats, including phishing and payment fraud, have also risen alongside digital adoption, disproportionately affecting first-time users.

A fully cashless economy must therefore be approached gradually and inclusively. Continued investment in digital infrastructure, vernacular-language interfaces, and sustained financial literacy campaigns are essential to ensure no one is left behind. Cash should remain available as a fallback option, particularly for vulnerable populations, even as digital adoption deepens.

In conclusion, while a cashless economy promises efficiency, transparency and inclusion, its success ultimately depends on building trust, accessibility, and safety nets alongside the technology itself.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Role of Women in India's Banking Sector Growth."
questions.modelAnswer:
Women have played an increasingly significant role in shaping India's banking sector, both as employees and as customers driving financial inclusion. Today, women occupy leadership positions across public and private sector banks, bringing diverse perspectives to strategy, risk management, and customer service.

On the customer side, initiatives like the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana have brought millions of previously unbanked women into the formal financial system, often for the first time controlling their own savings independently. Self-help groups, frequently led by women, have further deepened financial inclusion in rural areas, fostering both economic independence and improved household decision-making power.

Banks have also recognised women as a distinct and valuable customer segment, designing tailored products such as women-focused savings accounts, lower-interest business loans for women entrepreneurs, and insurance schemes addressing specific life-stage needs. This targeted approach has helped narrow the historical gender gap in financial access.

Despite this progress, challenges remain. Women in rural areas often face social and logistical barriers to accessing banking services independently, and representation of women in senior banking leadership, while improving, remains below parity. Addressing these gaps requires continued investment in last-mile banking infrastructure, mentorship programmes for women employees, and culturally sensitive outreach for women customers.

In conclusion, women are no longer peripheral to banking sector growth but central to it, both as professionals shaping the industry and as customers whose financial empowerment strengthens the broader economy.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Cybersecurity Challenges in the Era of Digital Banking."
questions.modelAnswer:
As banking services increasingly shift to digital platforms, cybersecurity has emerged as one of the sector's most pressing challenges. The convenience of mobile and internet banking has been matched by a corresponding rise in sophisticated cyber threats, ranging from phishing and identity theft to large-scale ransomware attacks targeting core banking infrastructure.

Financial institutions are attractive targets due to the sheer volume of sensitive data and funds they manage. A single successful breach can compromise millions of customer records, cause significant financial loss, and severely damage public trust built over years. Smaller cooperative banks and regional players, often with limited cybersecurity budgets, are particularly vulnerable compared to larger institutions with dedicated security teams.

Customers themselves remain a critical vulnerability, as social engineering tactics like fake customer-care calls and phishing links continue to exploit limited digital literacy, particularly among first-time digital users and senior citizens. Regulators have responded with stricter mandates around data protection, multi-factor authentication, and rapid breach disclosure timelines, pushing banks to strengthen their defences.

Addressing this challenge requires a multi-layered approach: robust technical safeguards such as encryption and real-time fraud detection, continuous employee training to recognise emerging threats, and sustained customer education campaigns on safe digital practices. Collaboration between banks, regulators, and law enforcement is equally essential to track and disrupt organised cybercrime networks.

In conclusion, cybersecurity in digital banking is not a one-time fix but an ongoing commitment, requiring constant vigilance as both technology and threats continue to evolve.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Sustainable Development: The Role of Banks in a Greener Economy."
questions.modelAnswer:
As the urgency of addressing climate change grows, banks are increasingly recognised as key enablers of sustainable development, given their central role in allocating capital across the economy. Through green lending, banks can direct credit towards renewable energy, energy-efficient infrastructure, and environmentally responsible businesses, while curbing financing for highly polluting industries.

Many Indian banks have begun issuing green bonds to raise dedicated funding for environmentally beneficial projects, while also incorporating climate-related risks into their credit assessment frameworks. This recognises that extreme weather events and shifting regulations can directly affect borrowers' ability to repay loans, particularly in climate-sensitive sectors like agriculture.

Beyond lending, banks influence sustainability through their own operations, including reducing paper usage via digital banking, adopting energy-efficient branch infrastructure, and setting internal carbon reduction targets. Some institutions have also begun offering sustainability-linked loans, where interest rates are tied to a borrower's environmental performance, creating direct financial incentives for greener business practices.

However, challenges remain. The absence of standardised green taxonomies makes it difficult to consistently classify and compare environmentally beneficial projects across institutions, raising concerns about superficial or exaggerated sustainability claims. Building genuine expertise in assessing environmental risk also requires significant investment in staff training and data systems.

In conclusion, banks occupy a uniquely influential position in the transition to a sustainable economy. By embedding environmental considerations into core lending and operational decisions, the sector can meaningfully accelerate, rather than merely accommodate, India's progress towards its climate commitments.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Impact of Social Media on Banking and Customer Engagement."
questions.modelAnswer:
Social media has fundamentally transformed how banks engage with customers, shifting communication from one-way notices to interactive, real-time conversations. Platforms once used purely for marketing are now key channels for customer service, financial education, and even product launches, reflecting how deeply social media has embedded itself in banking strategy.

For customers, social media offers a convenient avenue to raise queries or complaints publicly, often prompting faster resolution than traditional channels due to reputational considerations. Banks have responded by setting up dedicated social media response teams to handle queries swiftly and professionally. Educational content, including short videos explaining fraud prevention or new digital features, has also proven effective in reaching younger, digitally native audiences.

However, this shift carries risks. Misinformation or rumours about a bank's financial health can spread rapidly on social media, potentially triggering unwarranted panic among customers. Sensitive customer complaints discussed publicly can also expose private financial details if not handled carefully. Additionally, fraudsters increasingly exploit social media to run fake customer-care accounts, deceiving unsuspecting customers into sharing confidential information.

Banks must therefore balance the engagement benefits of social media with robust risk management: verified official accounts, rapid rumour-correction protocols, and clear guidance to customers on identifying genuine bank communication. Staff handling these platforms also require specific training in both customer service and crisis communication.

In conclusion, social media has become an indispensable tool for modern banking engagement, but its effective use demands as much caution and governance as it does creativity and responsiveness.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Financial Literacy: The Need of the Hour."
questions.modelAnswer:
As financial products grow increasingly complex and digital banking becomes the norm, financial literacy has emerged as an essential life skill rather than a specialised one. Despite expanding access to bank accounts through initiatives like the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, many account holders remain unaware of how to use these accounts meaningfully for savings, credit, or insurance.

Low financial literacy has tangible consequences. Individuals without a basic understanding of interest rates, loan terms, or investment risk are more vulnerable to predatory lending, unsuitable insurance products, and financial fraud. The rapid rise of digital payment fraud, often targeting first-time digital users unfamiliar with safe practices, further underscores this gap.

Financial literacy also has a broader economic dimension. A population that understands budgeting, saving, and responsible borrowing contributes to greater household financial stability and, by extension, a more resilient economy less vulnerable to debt-driven crises. Women and rural populations, historically underserved in financial education, stand to benefit disproportionately from targeted literacy efforts.

Addressing this gap requires a coordinated, multi-pronged approach: integrating financial education into school curricula from an early age, leveraging Business Correspondents and self-help groups for grassroots outreach, and designing simplified, vernacular-language digital tools that make financial concepts more accessible. Banks themselves have a role to play through ongoing customer education, not merely product promotion.

In conclusion, financial literacy is no longer optional in an increasingly complex financial landscape. Sustained, inclusive investment in financial education is essential to ensuring that expanded access to banking truly translates into financial well-being for all.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Start-up India: The Role of Banks in Nurturing Entrepreneurship."
questions.modelAnswer:
India's start-up ecosystem has grown remarkably over the past decade, supported by government initiatives such as Start-up India, an expanding pool of skilled talent, and increasing venture capital interest. Within this ecosystem, banks occupy a uniquely important, if traditionally cautious, role in providing the credit infrastructure that early-stage ventures need to scale.

Historically, banks have been reluctant lenders to start-ups, given the absence of tangible collateral, unproven business models, and higher failure rates compared to established businesses. This has pushed many start-ups towards equity financing, often diluting founder ownership earlier than ideal. However, this dynamic is gradually shifting, with banks introducing venture debt products, collateral-free credit guarantee schemes, and specialised start-up banking verticals designed to better understand and serve this segment.

Beyond direct lending, banks support entrepreneurship through ancillary services such as current account solutions tailored to start-up cash-flow patterns, payment gateway integrations, and advisory support on financial structuring. Partnerships between banks and start-up incubators have also become increasingly common, providing founders with both mentorship and easier access to formal credit channels.

Challenges remain, however. Risk assessment models calibrated for traditional businesses often do not translate well to evaluating start-ups, where intangible assets like intellectual property or user base may matter more than physical collateral. Building genuine expertise in start-up risk evaluation requires sustained investment in specialised underwriting capabilities.

In conclusion, as India's start-up ecosystem matures, deeper and more sophisticated bank participation could meaningfully reduce founders' dependence on equity dilution, strengthening both individual ventures and the broader entrepreneurial economy.

questions.text:
Write an essay in about 250-300 words on the following topic:

"Climate Change: A Growing Responsibility for the Banking Sector."
questions.modelAnswer:
Climate change is no longer a peripheral concern for banks but a central risk factor shaping lending decisions, regulatory expectations, and long-term institutional strategy. As extreme weather events grow more frequent, banks face direct exposure through borrowers in climate-vulnerable sectors such as agriculture, real estate, and infrastructure, whose ability to repay loans can be significantly disrupted by floods, droughts, or cyclones.

Recognising this, regulators including the Reserve Bank of India have begun encouraging banks to factor climate-related financial risks into their credit assessment frameworks, treating environmental risk with the same seriousness traditionally reserved for credit or market risk. Banks have responded by developing climate risk disclosure frameworks and stress-testing loan portfolios against various climate scenarios.

Beyond risk management, banks also bear a proactive responsibility to channel capital towards climate solutions. Green bonds, concessional financing for renewable energy projects, and sustainability-linked loans that reward environmentally responsible business practices are increasingly common tools through which banks can actively support, rather than merely accommodate, the transition to a low-carbon economy.

Challenges persist, however. The absence of standardised methodologies for measuring climate risk makes consistent assessment difficult, and smaller institutions often lack the technical expertise or data infrastructure to model these risks effectively. Balancing near-term commercial pressures against long-term climate responsibility also remains a genuine institutional tension.

In conclusion, climate change has fundamentally expanded what responsible banking means. Institutions that proactively integrate environmental considerations into both risk management and lending strategy will be better positioned for long-term resilience in an increasingly climate-constrained economy.

questions.text:

By offering small, unsecured loans through group-lending models, these institutions have enabled millions of rural entrepreneurs, particularly women, to start small businesses. (c) The sector has been criticised for high interest rates and aggressive recovery practices in some cases.

(e) Transformative.

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Managing inflation remains one of the Reserve Bank of India's primary mandates, achieved largely through adjustments to the repo rate, the rate at which it lends to commercial banks. Raising the repo rate makes borrowing costlier, typically cooling consumer spending and moderating inflation. However, this approach risks slowing economic growth and increasing EMI burdens on existing borrowers. The Monetary Policy Committee must therefore carefully balance inflation control against growth objectives, basing decisions on indicators including food prices, global crude oil trends, and currency stability."

questions.modelAnswer:

(d) Food prices and global crude oil trends (or currency stability).

questions.text:

However, specialised start-up financing schemes, venture debt products, and credit guarantee mechanisms are gradually emerging to bridge this gap. (d) It could reduce start-ups' dependence on equity dilution for early-stage capital.

questions.text:

"Cybersecurity has become a critical concern for banks as digital transactions surge. Threats range from phishing attacks to ransomware targeting core banking systems, and a single breach can compromise millions of customer records, eroding decades of public trust. Regulators worldwide are mandating stricter data protection standards, regular audits, and rapid breach disclosure timelines. However, technology alone cannot solve this challenge; employee awareness, customer education, and organisational vigilance are equally essential to building genuine cyber resilience."

(d) Employee awareness, customer education, and organisational vigilance.
(e) Resilience.

questions.text:

"Self-help groups have emerged as a powerful vehicle for women's financial empowerment in rural India, enabling members to pool savings and access collective credit without traditional collateral. Beyond financial inclusion, these groups have fostered leadership skills and greater participation of women in household decision-making. Banks have increasingly partnered with SHGs through linkage programmes, extending credit at favourable terms based on the group's savings track record. However, sustaining group cohesion and ensuring equitable benefit distribution remain ongoing challenges."

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"Non-performing assets have long posed a challenge to the health of India's banking sector, tying up capital that could otherwise support fresh lending. The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has provided a faster, more structured route for resolving stressed assets compared to earlier mechanisms. Banks have also strengthened early-warning systems to flag stressed accounts before they turn into full defaults. While gross NPA ratios have improved meaningfully from their peak, experts caution that asset quality must be watched closely."

questions.text:

"Artificial intelligence is increasingly deployed in banking customer service, with chatbots handling a growing share of routine queries, reducing wait times and costs while freeing staff for complex issues. However, concerns remain about AI misunderstanding nuanced grievances or responding impersonally to sensitive matters such as fraud or financial distress. Banks are therefore adopting hybrid models, where AI handles routine queries but escalates complex or emotionally sensitive cases to trained human representatives."

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the questions that follow in your own words.

"India's gig economy has expanded rapidly, with millions earning through platform-based work such as ride-hailing and food delivery. While this offers flexibility and income opportunities, gig workers typically lack access to formal employment benefits such as provident fund and health insurance. Their irregular income patterns also make it difficult to access traditional credit products designed for salaried employees. Financial institutions are now exploring tailored products, including flexible savings schemes and portable social security benefits, to better serve this workforce."

questions.text: Write an email (150-200 words) to a senior citizen customer to educate them about common banking frauds and how to stay safe.

Common frauds to watch out for:
1. KYC Fraud: Fraudsters call posing as bank staff asking for OTP or Aadhaar. Never share these.

- Always verify callers claiming to be from a bank.

questions.text: You are a PO at an SBI branch. A young customer comes to you crying, saying she was cheated by an online job portal that took Rs. 15,000 from her as a registration fee. She wants you to reverse the payment. How will you handle the situation? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
This is a possible cyber fraud case. I would first ensure the customer is calm, seated, and listened to carefully.

I would explain that the bank cannot directly reverse a transferred payment, but she has strong options:

1. Cybercrime Complaint: I would advise her to file a complaint at www.cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930 (National Cybercrime Helpline) immediately. Quick reporting can lead to freezing of the fraudster's account.
2. Police FIR: I would recommend filing a written complaint at the nearest police station under the IT Act.
3. Bank Dispute: I would help her fill a transaction dispute form, which will be sent to our fraud monitoring team. If the receiving account is found fraudulent, it may be frozen and partial or full recovery may be possible.
4. Documentation: I would ask her to preserve screenshots, payment receipts, and details of the job portal as evidence.
5. Awareness: I would gently advise her that genuine employers never charge registration fees — this is a well-known fraud pattern.

I would issue her a written acknowledgement of her complaint and follow up on the dispute internally.

Ensuring the customer leaves the branch feeling heard, supported, and with clear next steps is equally as important as the procedural response.

questions.text: As a PO at SBI, you discover that a colleague has been accepting cash gifts from customers in exchange for faster loan processing. What steps would you take? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
Accepting gifts from customers for preferential service is a clear violation of SBI's code of conduct and constitutes corruption. As a PO, I have a moral and professional duty to act.

1. Verify the Information: Before taking any action, I would ensure what I observed or heard is factual and not a misunderstanding. I would not accuse anyone based on unverified information.

2. Do Not Confront Directly: I would avoid confronting the colleague, as this could lead to denial or destruction of evidence.

3. Report in Writing: I would report the matter in writing to the Branch Manager with specific details — dates, nature of transactions, and any amounts known. This creates an official record.

4. Use Whistleblower Channels: SBI has an internal vigilance mechanism and whistleblower policy. I would use this to protect confidentiality and ensure proper investigation.

5. Escalate if Needed: If the Branch Manager appears complicit or inactive, I would escalate directly to SBI's Chief Vigilance Officer (CVO).

6. Maintain Confidentiality: I would not discuss the matter with other colleagues to ensure a fair process.

Maintaining integrity in a public sector bank is everyone's responsibility. I would act with professionalism and courage, keeping the bank's reputation and our customers' interests first.

questions.text: During peak hours, a customer is found unconscious inside your SBI branch. There is no doctor nearby. How would you manage the emergency? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
Finding a customer unconscious is a medical emergency requiring immediate, calm action.

1. Alert Staff: I would call out to branch staff and security to assist, and ask them to keep other customers calm and away from the area.
2. Call Emergency Services: I would immediately dial 108 (ambulance) or 112 and provide the branch address and situation details.
3. Basic First Aid: If any staff member is trained in CPR or first aid, I would request them to assist the customer while we wait for the ambulance. I would ensure the customer is not moved unnecessarily.
4. Identify the Customer: I would try to identify the customer using branch records or information from other customers present.
5. Contact Family: Using the emergency contact number in the customer's bank record, I would inform the family immediately.
6. Cooperate with Medical Team: On arrival, I would provide full cooperation and all available identification information.
7. Document the Incident: I would record the incident with the time, actions taken, and outcome in the branch register and report it to the Branch Manager.

The welfare of every customer inside our branch is our responsibility. Quick, calm, and coordinated action can save a life.

questions.text: A customer insists on withdrawing Rs. 2 lakhs in cash without following proper verification procedures, arguing they have been a customer for 20 years. How do you handle this? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
This situation requires balancing respect for a long-standing customer with adherence to banking regulations.

I would greet the customer warmly and thank them for their loyalty. I would then explain clearly and politely that verification procedures are not a sign of distrust — they are mandatory regulatory requirements that apply to all customers equally.

Reasons I would explain:
1. RBI Guidelines: Cash withdrawals above a certain limit require identity verification and may need to be reported under FEMA and Income Tax rules.
2. Customer Protection: These procedures protect the customer from fraud — someone else could attempt to impersonate them.
3. Bank Compliance: Skipping verification could expose the bank to legal and regulatory penalties.

I would request the customer to present valid photo ID (Aadhaar or PAN), fill the withdrawal slip, and allow OTP-based verification if required.

If the customer remains upset, I would offer to connect them with the Branch Manager. I would remain polite and never argue.

I would also note the interaction in the grievance register as a precaution.

In banking, procedure is non-negotiable regardless of relationship history. However, how we enforce it must always be respectful and empathetic.

questions.text: A group of 10 farmers enters the branch to inquire about PM Kisan Samman Nidhi and how to link their accounts. Most speak only a local dialect. How do you assist them? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
This situation highlights the importance of inclusive banking. As a PO, it is my duty to make banking accessible to all.

1. Welcome the Group: I would welcome them warmly and seat them in a designated corner to avoid disrupting other customers.
2. Language: I would identify a bilingual staff member to help interpret, or communicate in simple Hindi and the local language as best I can.
3. Explain PM Kisan: I would explain in simple terms that the government provides Rs. 6,000 per year in three instalments to small farmers, credited directly to their bank accounts linked with Aadhaar.
4. Assist with Linking:
   - Check if Aadhaar is already linked via the CBS system.
   - For those without Aadhaar linking, guide them to fill the Aadhaar seeding form.
   - Assist those without bank accounts to fill account opening forms.
5. Advise them to check PM Kisan status at pmkisan.gov.in.
6. Follow-up: I would note their contact numbers for future follow-up.

I would also provide pamphlets in the local language. Helping this group builds trust in the banking system and supports the government's financial inclusion mission.

questions.text: A customer reports receiving SMS alerts for transactions they did not make. They suspect their account has been hacked. As duty PO, what immediate steps would you take? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
A suspected account hack is a serious cyber security emergency. I would act swiftly and systematically.

1. Verify the Complaint: I would immediately pull up the customer's account on CBS and confirm whether the transactions in the SMS alerts are showing as unauthorised.
2. Block Account/Card: If suspicious transactions are confirmed, I would initiate a temporary block on the account or debit card to prevent further loss and ask the customer to change their net banking password and mobile PIN immediately.
3. Record the Complaint: I would formally record details of the unauthorised transactions — amounts, dates, and recipient details.
4. Report Internally: I would escalate to SBI's Fraud Risk Management team via the prescribed internal channel.
5. Advise Cybercrime Filing: I would ask the customer to file a complaint at www.cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930 for legal protection.
6. Reassure the Customer: I would explain that under RBI guidelines, the bank's liability for unauthorised transactions reported promptly is limited, and they may be eligible for a refund.

The faster a suspected hack is reported and contained, the better the chance of protecting the customer's funds.

questions.text: You are the only PO on duty and the branch is extremely busy. A visually impaired customer arrives needing help to fill out a demand draft form. How would you ensure they are assisted without neglecting other customers? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
Assisting a visually impaired customer while managing a busy branch requires empathy and time management.

1. Acknowledge First: I would immediately acknowledge the customer, make them feel welcome, and gently guide them to a seat near my counter.
2. Manage the Queue: I would delegate routine tasks to available tellers and request the security guard to inform waiting customers of a brief delay.
3. Assist with the DD Form: I would personally help the customer by:
   - Asking for the required details (beneficiary name, amount, payable city)
   - Filling the form on their behalf
   - Reading all filled details back to them clearly for confirmation
   - Guiding their hand to the signature/thumb impression area
   - Ensuring the DD is processed and handing them the receipt
4. Inform About Accessible Banking: I would tell them about SBI's screen-reader enabled YONO app and toll-free customer care (1800-11-2211).

Banking must be inclusive and accessible. No customer — especially one with a disability — should feel like a burden. Equal and dignified service is not optional — it is our duty.

questions.text: A young man enters the branch and starts recording a video of the banking hall, claiming he is a social media influencer reviewing the branch experience. Other customers appear uncomfortable. What would you do? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
This situation requires balancing respect for the individual with the privacy rights of customers and bank security.

1. Approach Politely: I would approach the individual with a polite smile and introduce myself. I would not be confrontational.
2. Explain the Concern clearly:
   - Recording inside a bank without permission may capture sensitive customer information (account details, PINs, documents).
   - It is a security concern and against bank policy.
   - Other customers have a right to privacy.
3. Offer an Alternative: I would request him to stop recording and suggest that if he genuinely wants to do a review, he may speak to the Branch Manager to obtain written permission. I would offer to answer his questions about our services personally.
4. If He Refuses: I would ask the security guard to intervene politely and, if necessary, request him to leave the premises.
5. Document the Incident: After the event, I would note it in the branch register and inform the Branch Manager.

Handling this with courtesy protects the bank's reputation while ensuring the safety and comfort of all customers.

questions.text: A customer arrives at your branch very angry, shouting that they have been waiting for 45 minutes and threatening to write to the banking ombudsman. As duty PO, how will you defuse the situation? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
Handling an angry customer requires patience, empathy, and calm communication.

1. Stay Calm: I would approach the customer composedly. I would not react defensively or match their anger.
2. Apologise Sincerely: A simple genuine apology defuses tension: 'Sir/Madam, I sincerely apologise for the long wait. I want to help you right away. What do you need assistance with today?'
3. Attend Promptly: I would attend to their query without further delay, giving them priority service as a goodwill gesture. If appropriate, I would briefly and honestly explain the reason for the delay — not as an excuse, but as information.
4. Address the Ombudsman Mention: I would not discourage them. I would acknowledge their right to escalate if unsatisfied and provide the Banking Ombudsman's contact details, along with our branch's grievance form.
5. Record and Improve: After the interaction, I would log the incident in the branch grievance register and inform the Branch Manager to review queue management procedures.

A difficult customer handled well often becomes a loyal customer. Every complaint is an opportunity to improve.

questions.text: A borrower comes to your SBI branch requesting a six-month moratorium on their home loan EMI, citing job loss during the current economic slowdown. How would you handle this? (200-250 words)
questions.modelAnswer:
Job loss is a genuine hardship. I would handle this with both compassion and professional thoroughness.

1. Listen First: I would invite the customer to sit, listen to their full situation, and assure them that the bank has processes to help borrowers in genuine distress.
2. Explain Available Options:
   - Formal Moratorium: If a government or RBI moratorium scheme is active, I would check eligibility and guide the application process.
   - Loan Restructuring: Even without a formal scheme, SBI has internal restructuring options such as extending the tenure to reduce the monthly EMI burden.
3. Documentation Required: I would inform the customer that proof of job loss (resignation/termination letter), salary slips, and bank statements are required to support the request.
4. Be Transparent: I would explain that interest continues to accrue during a moratorium period and that it may be reported to credit bureaus, potentially affecting their CIBIL score. This is important for them to know before deciding.
5. Refer for Decision: I would forward the application to the Branch Manager and Loan Department with a recommended timeline for response.

I would ensure the customer leaves with clarity, dignity, and confidence that the bank takes their situation seriously.

questions.text: Write a report (200-250 words) to your Branch Manager on a Mobile Banking Awareness Camp conducted at a rural village near your branch.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON MOBILE BANKING AWARENESS CAMP

To: The Branch Manager, SBI
From: [Name], Probationary Officer
Date: [Date]

questions.text:
Read the following passage (approximately 300 words) carefully and write a precis in 80-100 words. Cover all main points in your own words.

The concept of a green economy has gained significant momentum in recent years as governments, businesses, and citizens become more aware of the environmental challenges facing the planet. A green economy is broadly defined as an economic system that aims to reduce environmental risks and ecological scarcity, and promote sustainable development without damaging the environment. It focuses on low carbon energy, resource efficiency, and social inclusiveness. Unlike conventional economic models that prioritise short-term profit and GDP growth, the green economy takes into account the long-term costs of environmental degradation, climate change, and natural resource depletion.

Renewable energy is at the heart of the green economy. Solar, wind, and hydropower are replacing fossil fuels in many countries, reducing greenhouse gas emissions and dependence on finite energy sources. India, for instance, has set an ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030. Green building technologies, electric vehicles, and sustainable agriculture are other pillars of this new economic model.

A green economy also holds the promise of job creation. According to the International Labour Organization, the transition to a greener economy could create over 24 million new jobs globally by 2030. However, this transition also comes with challenges. Workers in fossil fuel industries must be retrained and reskilled to participate in the green economy, a process known as just transition. Policymakers must ensure that the shift to cleaner technologies does not leave behind vulnerable communities that currently depend on carbon-intensive sectors.

The role of finance is also critical. Green bonds, climate funds, and ESG investing are emerging as powerful tools to direct capital toward sustainable projects. Without adequate and well-targeted investment, the green economy cannot take root at the scale needed to address the climate crisis effectively.

Write your precis below (80-100 words):
questions.modelAnswer: A green economy aims to reduce environmental risks and promote sustainable development through low-carbon energy, resource efficiency, and social inclusiveness. It differs from conventional models by considering long-term environmental costs. Renewable energy, green buildings, electric vehicles, and sustainable agriculture are its key pillars. The transition can create 24 million new jobs globally, but workers in fossil fuel sectors need reskilling. Finance plays a vital role, with green bonds and ESG investing directing capital toward sustainable projects. Targeted investment is essential for the green economy to address climate change effectively.

questions.text:
Read the following passage (approximately 300 words) carefully and write a precis in 80-100 words.

Mental health has long been treated as a secondary concern in public health policy, particularly in developing countries like India. For decades, physical ailments received the majority of healthcare funding and attention, while mental health issues were dismissed, stigmatised, or simply ignored. This has begun to change in recent years, with growing recognition that mental health is inseparable from overall wellbeing and economic productivity.

India faces an enormous mental health burden. According to a Lancet study, nearly 200 million Indians suffer from some form of mental illness, yet only about 3 in 1,000 people receive any form of mental health care. The treatment gap is staggering. A severe shortage of psychiatrists, psychologists, and counsellors, combined with widespread social stigma, means that most people with mental illness suffer in silence.

The COVID-19 pandemic dramatically worsened the mental health crisis. Lockdowns, job losses, social isolation, and fear of infection led to a sharp rise in anxiety, depression, and suicide rates across the country. It also brought mental health into public conversation in a way that had not happened before, leading to greater awareness and reduced stigma among younger generations.

The government has taken some steps through the National Mental Health Programme and the Tele-MANAS helpline launched in 2022, which provides free counselling services across the country. However, investment in mental health remains critically low — India spends less than 1% of its health budget on mental health. Experts argue that meaningful change requires not just more funding, but a cultural shift that normalises help-seeking behaviour and integrates mental health support into schools, workplaces, and primary healthcare centres.

Write your precis below (80-100 words):
questions.modelAnswer: Mental health has historically been neglected in India, with nearly 200 million people suffering from mental illness but very few receiving treatment. Severe shortages of mental health professionals and social stigma have widened this gap. The COVID-19 pandemic worsened the crisis while also raising public awareness. Government initiatives like the NMHP and Tele-MANAS helpline are positive steps, but India spends less than 1% of its health budget on mental health. Experts call for more investment and a cultural shift that normalises seeking mental health support in schools, workplaces, and healthcare centres.

questions.text:
Read the following passage (approximately 300 words) carefully and write a precis in 80-100 words.
